# Radio Group

Shows a set of mutually exclusive choices where one value can be selected.

## Installation

Add Radio Group from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/radio-group
```

## Usage

Use Radio Group when users should compare a small set of choices and select one. Give every `RadioGroupItem` a unique value and a visible Label associated through matching `for` and `id` values.

**Controlled radio group example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="grid gap-3">
    <RadioGroup v-model="density" orientation="vertical" name="density">
      <div class="flex items-center space-x-2">
        <RadioGroupItem id="default" value="default" />
        <Label for="default">Default</Label>
      </div>
      <div class="flex items-center space-x-2">
        <RadioGroupItem id="comfortable" value="comfortable" />
        <Label for="comfortable">Comfortable</Label>
      </div>
      <div class="flex items-center space-x-2">
        <RadioGroupItem id="compact" value="compact" />
        <Label for="compact">Compact</Label>
      </div>
    </RadioGroup>
    <p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      Selected: {{ density }}
    </p>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Label } from '@/Components/Label'
import {
  RadioGroup,
  RadioGroupItem,
} from '@/Components/RadioGroup'
import { ref } from 'vue'

const density = ref('comfortable')
</script>
```

## Controlled selection

Bind `v-model` when other interface state needs the selected value. Use `default-value` only for an initial uncontrolled choice. Add `name` when the group participates in native form submission and `required` when one choice must be made.

Do not preselect an option when a deliberate, unbiased choice is required. When a safe or common default exists, selecting it can reduce effort as long as the user can clearly change it.

## Orientation and keyboard behavior

Set `orientation="vertical"` for stacked options and `horizontal` only when labels remain short and the available width is stable. Reka manages focus and arrow-key navigation between items. The `loop` prop controls whether navigation wraps at the ends; avoid adding separate tab stops inside option labels.

## States and validation

Disable the root when no choice can be changed, or disable individual unavailable items while leaving the rest usable. Put `aria-invalid` on the relevant item or group behavior as appropriate, render an error message nearby, and retain the visible group question.

## Accessibility

Wrap a related set in `FieldSet` with `FieldLegend` so the question is announced with the options. Keep each option label clickable and avoid relying on visual position, color, or icons to distinguish choices.

## Choosing a control

> **Use Checkbox for independent choices: [Checkbox](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/checkbox)**
>
> If users may choose several options—or turn one independent option on and off—use Checkbox rather than Radio Group.

> **Use Select when space is constrained: [Select](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/select)**
>
> For a longer list or a compact layout where comparing every option is less important, place the choices in Select.

Hosted documentation: [Radio Group](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/radio-group)
